02 / 21 MYTHOS
01 THE PROBLEM

Local commerce is being extracted.

Three taxes. Same dollar. Every transaction.

01

Visibility Tax

Small businesses pay Google, Meta, and Yelp just to appear in their own neighborhoods. While Starbucks spent $306M on ads in 2023, 66% of small businesses spend under $1,000/year — competing against billion-dollar budgets.

02

Operations Tax

$500–$3,000+/month stitching together disconnected SaaS — booking, CRM, marketing, reviews, social, payroll — none of which talk to each other. The owner becomes the integration layer.

03

Transaction Tax

Visa and Mastercard take 2.7% of every transaction. Supplier payments, same thing. The same dollar gets taxed multiple times before it leaves the block.

THE RECEIPTS MYTHOS × UTD JINDAL SCHOOL · SPRING 2026
$1.451B
extracted from DFW + San Diego small businesses every year by Google, Meta, and Yelp combined.
ENOUGH TO FUND 39,628 FULL-TIME LOCAL JOBS — IF THE MONEY STAYED LOCAL
$112–187B
national annual extraction across 33M US small businesses paying three platforms just to be visible.
UTD JINDAL CAPSTONE · SPRING 2026
Featuring antitrust expert Matt Stoller, Director of Research at the American Economic Liberties Project. 157K+ X followers. BIG newsletter ~85K subscribers. Author of Goliath (Simon & Schuster). Bylines: NYT, Washington Post, Guardian, Politico, Foreign Policy, Fast Company, Vice.
03 / 21 MYTHOS
02 ONE OF THEM
SARAH · COFFEE SHOP OWNER · NORTH PARK, SD

Meet Sarah.

She owns a coffee shop in North Park.

Her coffee is better than Starbucks. Her space is better. Her music is better.

She still loses every month, and the reason has nothing to do with her coffee.

Sarah is not losing because her coffee is worse.
She is losing because the system is built to extract from her.

There are 33 million Sarahs in America.

04 / 21 MYTHOS
03 THE MISSION
We are not building software.
We are building the infrastructure to fight back.

Big Tech built one operating system for global commerce. Nobody built one for local commerce. We are.

MythOS gives every Sarah the operating system, the storefront, the discovery layer, and the payment rail to compete with monopolies on equal ground.

Chartered Public Benefit · Delaware PBC
"To rebuild equitable digital infrastructure for local communities by empowering small businesses and residents to discover, connect, and transact with one another free from algorithmic exploitation."
05 / 21 MYTHOS
04 MYTHOS PRO — THE FOUNDATION

The operating system every local business has been waiting for.

Starts at $100/month (+ Mylo token usage). Replaces $500–$4,300/month in disconnected tools.

What's inside

  • Mylo AI Receptionist, answers calls, books appointments, follows up with no-shows
  • Marketing Studio, designs and ships social posts, ads, campaigns
  • All-in-One CRM, unifies texts, emails, social DMs into one inbox
  • Business Analytics, what's working, what's not, what to do next
  • Team Management, scheduling, payroll, communication
  • POS Integration, Square, Toast, Clover

What it replaces

Owner.com$499/mo
HubSpot$500/mo
Birdeye + Podium$698/mo
Square Teams + Payroll$154/mo
Marketing agency$3,000+/mo
MythOS ProFrom $100/mo
Strategy
Vertical wedge today (barbershops + salons), horizontal infrastructure tomorrow. Same AI brain configures for coffee shops, gyms, restaurants, retail.
Market
33M US small businesses × ~$1,200/yr software spend = $40B TAM. 8M+ local service businesses as immediate target.

07 / 21 MYTHOS
05 MYTHOS MIRROR — SMART MIRROR FOR BARBERSHOPS & SALONS

A smart mirror at every chair.
Hardware lock-in for MythOS Pro.

What it does

  • Mylo recognizes the client by face, pulls style history
  • AI-generated photorealistic preview of any haircut on their face
  • Touchless gesture control — no fingerprints
  • Directional speaker — only the person in the chair hears Mylo
  • Entertainment mode between cuts (Netflix, ESPN, YouTube)
  • Every interaction syncs to MythOS Pro CRM in real time
Where we are
Hardware locked. Specced down to the component, sourced.
Manufacturing locked. China ODM through Said Shaban.
Distribution locked. Hundreds of shops, 100+ supplier stores, beauty schools, Fellow Barber + named flagship operators.

Bridge math

  • Batch 1: 25 units × ~$1,200 ODM cost = $30K out
  • Sold through Said's network at ~$2,500/unit = ~$50–60K back
  • Funds Batch 2 (50 units) from revenue
  • Hardware operation self-funding by end of bridge
The lock-in
Six MythOS mirrors at every chair = switching off MythOS is a hardware rip-out, not a software migration. That changes churn fundamentally.
Market
~2M addressable mirror-chairs in US barbershops + salons. $7–10B US, $40B+ globally. No incumbent. We are creating the category.
08 / 21 MYTHOS
06 THE NETWORK — WHY IT HAS TO EXIST

Meet Jake.

22 years old. Lives near a college. Been on his couch for 45 minutes. He wanted to do something tonight. He opened Instagram and got influencers in Dubai and ads he doesn't need.

JAKE · 22 · SAN DIEGO · DOOMSCROLLING

Jake would love Sarah's coffee shop.

He will never find her.

Global algorithms optimize for engagement, not connection. The system that should connect Jake to his city is the same system that keeps him alone in his apartment.

We are building the alternative.
Epidemic
WHO declares loneliness a global health priority (Nov 2023)
Director-General Tedros: "as lethal as smoking 15 cigarettes a day." First WHA resolution passed May 2025.
WORLD HEALTH ORGANIZATION · WHO COMMISSION ON SOCIAL CONNECTION
8.2 hrs
Average teen daily social media use
57% say they use it too much. 38% report negative experiences. JAMA Pediatrics 2024: cutting use to 30 min/day reduced loneliness in 3 weeks.
BOSTON CHILDREN'S DIGITAL WELLNESS LAB · JAMA PEDIATRICS · 2024
48,824
US suicide deaths in 2024 alone
One every 10.8 minutes. Second leading cause of death for ages 10–34. US Surgeon General: social isolation directly contributes.
CDC NATIONAL VITAL STATISTICS · AFSP · 2024 FINAL DATA
$2.15T
Big Tech combined revenue, 2024–2025
Amazon + Apple + Alphabet + Microsoft + Meta + NVIDIA. Larger than the GDP of Italy, Canada, or Brazil.
PUBLIC COMPANY FINANCIALS · MOST RECENT FY
Big Tech monetized $2.15 trillion off attention, alienation, and global feeds. We are building the antidote.

Zero-CAC distribution

Every business on The Network is a pre-qualified Pro lead. Sarah signs up free, gets Jake walking in, upgrades to Pro for more Jakes.

Hyperlocal data moat

Meta + Google optimize for global. We optimize for the block. The hyperlocal behavioral data we generate, they cannot replicate.

Mylo trains here

Every Network interaction makes Mylo smarter, per neighborhood, per city. The flywheel that makes every other surface more valuable.

Wallet's closed loop

Every transaction in The Network can settle through MythOS rails, at a fraction of card-network cost.

10 / 21 MYTHOS
07 MYLO
images/mylo-phone.png
Meet Mylo
The AI brain behind every MythOS surface. One assistant. Many surfaces. Trained on hyperlocal data.

One brain.
Many surfaces.

Mylo is not an app. Mylo is the AI brain that lives across every surface in a local economy. Sarah uses Mylo to run her business. Jake uses Mylo to navigate his city. Same brain, different surfaces.

Mylo for Operators

Lives in MythOS Pro. Receptionist, marketing, CRM, analytics, team management.

Mylo for Consumers

Lives in The Network. Personalized city assistant. $20/mo premium tier.

Mylo on the Mirror

Lives in the chair. Chair-side consultation, hairstyle preview, customer recognition.

Mylo in Glasses

Lives on the face. Ambient, hands-free, for both operators and consumers.

Every surface generates data. Every data point makes Mylo smarter. We do not monetize attention. We monetize intelligence.

08 MYLO GLASSES — THE PLATFORM ENDPOINT

Mylo Glasses.
AR-guided training. Hands-free AI. $899.

Single Pro-tier SKU. Custom MythOS-designed aviator frame, full in-lens display, on-device AI, split-temple battery, magnetic snap-in charging case. Recognizable from across a room. Not a clone of Ray-Ban Meta.

For the business that hires hourly workers

The biggest hidden cost in every local business is training. Mylo Glasses cut weeks of onboarding to days.

For everyday people on The Network

  • Walking past Sarah's shop, Mylo says "this is the spot you bookmarked"
  • At a farmers market, Mylo points to the vendor matching what you came for
  • At an event, Mylo surfaces Rebel points, friends nearby, the next thing happening
Market
Mirror: $7–10B US TAM. Glasses: $120B+ US ceiling. Multi-trillion globally. 10–30% of US adults wearing smart glasses within a decade.

Meta did the hardware hard work. We put MythOS on top — the platform built for local commerce.
14 / 21 MYTHOS
09 MYTHOS WALLET — THE ECONOMIC LAYER

MythOS Wallet.
0.7% transactions. Account-to-account.

Visa / Mastercard
2.7%
Merchant fee per swipe. Standard interchange + assessment + processor margin.
MythOS Wallet
0.7%
Modeled on EPI / Wero. Account-to-account, bypassing card networks entirely.
The Proof

EPI / Wero is live across 13 European countries with 130M users. Real eCommerce processing today.

Interchange savings

2% savings split between merchant, partner bank, and MythOS.

Float yield share

Same model as Cash App's banking partner. Revenue share on yield.

Closed-loop economics

Myth Credits. 1:1 USD-pegged. Rebel points, breakage, lift. Merchant-to-merchant settlement.

Architecture & Compliance
Same model Cash App uses with Sutton Bank. Account-to-account settlement bypasses card-network PCI scope. Partner bank holds FBO custody and regulated functions (BSA/AML, money transmitter licenses). Plaid (SOC 2 Type II) handles bank credentials. Architecture intentionally designed to launch without our own licenses — optionality preserved to pursue direct charter at scale if strategic.

Phase A · Lock-In

ODM partners signed. Engineering producing. Mirror Batch 1 in production. Sales pipeline in motion.

Phase B · Ship & Sell

6 mirrors at Minty. Batch 1 sells through. Pro web + mobile + POS + Network in App Stores. Glasses prototype delivered.

Phase C · Story

Big Tech Tax Report in major press. Fight for Local executed. 10+ video testimonials. AbuAurelius narrative in flight.

Phase D · Lock the Seed

Full ecosystem live. 75+ mirrors deployed. Validated unit economics. Press flywheel turning.

12 THE TEAM

The team building this.

Nate Adams
Founder & CEO
Former barber and MMA fighter. Built MythOS solo over the past year, leveraging AI agents as a force multiplier. Vision, product, technical leadership, fundraising. Full-time on MythOS, no other income.
"Most barbers talked sports with their clients. I talked business strategy with founders and CEOs for years. Taking on Big Tech requires someone who is used to fighting uphill battles."
Said Shaban
Co-Founder Track · Head of Hardware & Industry
Owner of Minty Barber (MythOS pilot, two DFW locations). Decade-plus in hair and beauty industry. China ODM manufacturing relationships. Direct distribution into Fellow Barber, Blind Barber, beauty schools, supplier stores, hundreds of shops across DFW and nationally. Transitioning from Minty into full-time MythOS as the company scales.
DaMarco
Head of Sales
Institutional sales lead. Building chamber, BID, and franchise pipelines. Base only with equity ramp tied to MRR + partnership milestones.
Founder-Market Fit
This is the AI Square structurally cannot build. They have to serve everyone. We are the customer.
The Unfair Advantage Layer
AbuAurelius brand reach UTDSolv research relationship Americas Chamber president Mike Wilkes / Plug and Play McKinney Innovation Fund $200K qualified UTD engineering + fintech pipelines Internal AI workforce — proprietary multi-agent system powering company ops at zero payroll cost
